iconicCombos.pair: Pro Staff RF97 Autograph + Wilson Natural Gut / Luxilon ALU Power
iconicCombos.why: Heavy player's frame, gut mains for feel, poly crosses for control. Classic touch-game hybrid.
Heavy topspin frame matched with the spin-king poly. Built for monster lasso forehands.

iconicCombos.pair: Speed Pro 2024 + Babolat VS Touch / Head Hawk hybrid
iconicCombos.why: Control frame, gut mains for arm + dwell time, poly crosses for bite and durability.
Modern pro topspin standard — explosive frame plus the most-used pro string on tour.
The gentlest combo: flexible frame + soft multifilament. Great for tennis elbow.
Spin-shaped frame + control-poly with great snapback. Spin and shape on every ball.
The most popular tour combo — predictable response, refined feel, plenty of plow-through.
Tighter pattern + control-poly = ropey flat drives. Great for redirection and depth.

racketBlurbs.R001: Federer's signature blade — 12.5 oz of heavy, head-light precision with a thin beam that lets you feel every contact.
racketBlurbs.R002: The standard Pro Staff 97 — same refined Pro Staff DNA as the RF, slightly easier to swing all day.
racketBlurbs.R003: The Pro Staff in lighter trim — Pro Staff feel without needing tour-level swing speed; surprisingly arm-friendly.
racketBlurbs.R004: The most-played frame on the ATP tour — clean pocketing, an open pattern that grabs the ball, and that famous Blade flex.
racketBlurbs.R005: Same Blade chassis with a tighter 18×20 pattern — flatter trajectory, more first-strike control, less spin off the layup.
racketBlurbs.R006: The big-head Blade — 104 sq in and only 10.7 oz, with the same buttery RA 62 flex but a forgiving sweet spot.
racketBlurbs.R007: Easy power baseliner frame — head-heavy bias and stiff layup that punches above its weight on serves and groundies.
racketBlurbs.R008: The most flexible frame in the lineup at RA 55 — bends in your hand. Best-in-class arm comfort, period.
racketBlurbs.R009: The heavier Clash — keeps the signature flex but adds plow-through for advancing players. Comfort plus stability.
racketBlurbs.R010: The 18×16 spin pattern — fewer mains, more snapback, gnarly RPMs. Built for the modern aggressive baseliner.
racketBlurbs.R011: The all-time bestseller — RA 72, free power across the stringbed, alive on contact. The default for a generation.
racketBlurbs.R012: Pure Drive in extended length (27.5″) — bigger leverage on serves and groundstrokes for tall players.
racketBlurbs.R013: Tighter, more advanced Pure Drive — keeps the trampoline feel but rewards better technique with more control.
racketBlurbs.R014: Nadal's frame — the modern spin standard. Beefy 26mm beam and an open 16×19 that produces topspin from contact.
racketBlurbs.R015: The smaller, more refined Aero — Pure Aero spin in a frame that punches through the court, head-light and sharp.
racketBlurbs.R016: Extended Pure Aero (27.5″) — bigger leverage on the serve while keeping that signature spin-friendly response.
racketBlurbs.R017: Control-spin hybrid — less powerful than a Pure Drive, more spin-friendly than a Blade. Lives in a uniquely useful middle.
racketBlurbs.R018: The tightly-strung 18×20 Pure Strike — built for precision flat-ball strikers; plows through when you commit to the shot.
racketBlurbs.R019: The throwback player's frame — modern construction, classic geometry, 10-point head-light. For the all-courter who values feel.
racketBlurbs.R020: Djokovic's family in MP trim — lightning-quick through the air and the most balanced frame Head makes.
racketBlurbs.R021: Closest available to Djokovic's actual setup — 12 oz, head-light, 18×20 pattern. Tour-level control and stability.
racketBlurbs.R022: The lightweight Speed — faster swings, bigger sweet spot, easier shoulder load. Speed at its friendliest.
racketBlurbs.R023: The most flexible player's frame in the DB — RA 61, 95 head, old-school feel from modern materials.
racketBlurbs.R024: The most demanding frame here — 12.4 oz, 11-point head-light, RA 60. Reserved for pros who hit with full strokes.
racketBlurbs.R025: The all-court Head — sits between the faster Speed and the heavier Prestige. Solid pick for advancing intermediates.
racketBlurbs.R026: Head's spin frame — 26mm beam, RA 68, open pattern. Stiff feel, big spin and power for aggressive baseliners.
racketBlurbs.R027: Easy power for steady players — sits in the Pure Drive zone; gives free pace without demanding fast technique.
racketBlurbs.R028: Bridge frame — Head's MXG fusion tech feels both stiff and damped. Niche but distinct.
racketBlurbs.R029: Yonex's most-played control-tweener — buttery feel and the isometric head shape that adds a real sweet-spot bump.
racketBlurbs.R030: The 100-head EZONE — friendly access to spin and power. Yonex's answer to the Pure Drive / Speed MP space.
racketBlurbs.R031: Lighter EZONE 98 — same control character with less mass. Junior-friendly and great for senior players who want EZONE feel.
racketBlurbs.R032: Yonex's spin frame — open pattern, RA 66, snappier than a Pure Aero in a smaller head. For aggressive shape-makers.
racketBlurbs.R033: VCORE in 100 trim — slightly easier to use than the 98, blending spin and power for spin-curious intermediates.
racketBlurbs.R034: Replaced the VCORE Pro — RA 59, the most flexible Yonex player's frame. For advanced all-courters who chase feel.
racketBlurbs.R035: Percept 100 — flexible all-court 100, sits between an EZONE (tweener) and VCORE (control). Versatile and arm-friendly.
racketBlurbs.R036: Tweener with a mid-tier price tag — solid pickup for intermediates without paying flagship money.
racketBlurbs.R037: Old-school Phantom — flexible RA 60 with an 18×20 pattern. Cult favorite among advanced flat-ball strikers.
racketBlurbs.R038: Phantom in 16×18 — slight spin upgrade over the 100X with the same flexible RA 60 chassis.
racketBlurbs.R039: Spanish-made player's frame — tour-grade feel at a more accessible weight; sneaky-good value.
racketBlurbs.R040: The popular TFight — control-bias 11.6 oz frame. Medvedev-style precision feel without the price tag.
racketBlurbs.R041: Lighter TFight — 10.9 oz, easy to swing, refined feel. Friendly intermediate option.
racketBlurbs.R042: Tweener Tecnifibre — RA 66, more lively, power-leaning. For improving players who want some help.
racketBlurbs.R043: Dunlop's main control frame — plays a lot like a Blade with a different feel character, at a friendlier price.
racketBlurbs.R044: Control-tour CX — 12 oz, 18×20 pattern. For advanced players who want flat depth and predictable feedback.
racketBlurbs.R045: Dunlop's spin frame — 27mm beam, RA 68, generous power. Aggressive-baseliner-friendly.
racketBlurbs.R046: Dunlop's tweener — easier swing, free power. Modern beginner-friendly frame.
